mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, expanding valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.5% signals excess labor slack that depresses aggregate wage income and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong retail sales and contained consumer prices indicate robust real demand without imminent monetary tightening, jointly lifting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of high RSAFS and low CPIAUCSL fails to generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when housing starts remain above recessionary thresholds, confirming broad-based rather than narrow manufacturing expansion over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: broad-based expansion with stable construction activity
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO increases and HOUST is above 900 compared to when HOUST is below 900, using a t-test at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of tighter Federal Reserve policy, increasing real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate income and consumption capacity, which drives aggregate corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling 2% below its trailing 12-month peak signals a manufacturing recession that compresses corporate profit margins and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when INDPRO declines 2% from its 12-month peak.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment combined with a low federal funds rate indicates a tightening labor market without offsetting monetary restraint, which raises expected future wage costs and compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: tightening labor market with accommodative policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE is falling and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.
